msgs.text:
Turn 54 — D.3 stack-rank certificate executed; the last verifier gap closes I used only the files attached to this message. The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the quotients ΓS,ΓH defined from F=P⋊G, with the required condition πH∘Φ=πS.

Turn 53 left exactly one executable gap: the residual continuation matrix D5,6,7 had been reproduced from actual restricted representatives with rankD5,6,7=60,but the coefficient-Bockstein rowμ4B4:3L5⊕3L6⊕3L7→F2 had not yet been computed over the first 2-coefficient quotient A2=R/(4,t). The remaining target was the stack-rank certificate rank(D5,6,7μ4B4)=61,because H4(2)is one-dimensional and this rank jump is exactly the invariant statement that the continuation kernel toggles μ4.

New result

The A2 -coefficient row is now computed, and the rank jump is verified:rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7μ4B4)=61.So the D.3 certificate is now executable, not merely structural.

I wrote the verifier here:Download turn54_d3_stackrank_verifier.py

Its output is:dim actual reps L1..L8: [2, 3, 2, 6, 6, 11, 18, 36]base residual terms degrees 1..8: [0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0]D_5,6,7 columns: 105 rank(D_5,6,7): 60 rank(e0..e5): 6 mu-transfer functional weight: 4 fixed A2 f5 equals beta4(e0+e2+e3+e4,0,0): True fixed mu4: 1 weight(mu4 B4 row): 30 rank([D_5,6,7 ; mu4 B4]): 61 What was computed The verifier keeps the residual and coefficient parts separate, as Turn 53 said was necessary. The residual part builds the actual restricted representatives and the fixed I9 -base, then recomputesS(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))X−2 ≡1(modI9)and rankD5,6,7=60.This matches the Turn 53 representative-dependent residual calculation.

The new part is the A2=R/(4,t) ghost evaluator. It works over Z/4 truncated to the degree needed for the coefficient-2, degree-5 lower ghost. The degree-four lower ghost map isβ4(u0,u1,uq)=[B,u0]+[A,u1]+[B,uq],and the scalar detecting the one-dimensional quotient H4(2)isμ4=coeffe1[2]+coeffe3[2].

The script constructs a transfer functional ℓ:T5→F2 satisfying ℓ∘β4=μ4.

Then, for each of the 105 continuation columns in 3L5⊕3L6⊕3L7, it computes the actual Z/4 coefficient-2 degree-5 ghost and applies ℓ. That gives the row μ4B4.

The fixed A2 -base sanity check also matches the earlier structural certificate:f5=β4(e0+e2+e3+e4,0,0),and the corresponding square coordinates are (1,0,1,1,1,0),soμ4=1.This is exactly the false-looking fixed scalar identified earlier; the new stack-rank computation proves it is killed by the continuation kernel, because μ4B4 is not in the row span of D5,6,7. The structural history of this D.3 issue was that the fixed scalar μ4=1 is non-invariant and is toggled by the degree 5,6,7 continuation fiber.

Consequence

The rank jump 60⟶61 means there exists κ∈kerD5,6,7 such thatμ4B4 (κ)=1.

Thus the fixed value μ4=1 can be retuned to μ4=0. This is precisely the D.3 certificate needed for the first A2=R/(4,t), d=4 secondary coefficient obstruction.

So the nonlinear certificate ledger is now fully closed:blockD.1D.2D.3D.4 content ker∂8↠L5[2]residual degree-16 λ-toggleA2, d=4, μ4 -toggleθ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]status explicit witnesses explicit lower-base witness stack-rank 60→61explicit quotient witness

Turn 48 had already supplied D.4, showing that the residual d=8 codimension-one miss disappears after quotienting by imθ80.The only missing item after Turn 53 was D.3’s executable μ4B4 row, and that is now done.This does not change the structural proof of the isomorphism; it strengthens the reproducibility layer supporting it. The earlier compactness proof uses finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms, and passes to an inverse-limit over-G automorphism sending rS−1 to rH, hence descending to ΓS≅GΓH.

ESTABLISHED: The final D.3 executable certificate is verified. The A2=R/(4,t) coefficient-Bockstein row μ4B4 has been computed from actual Z/4 coefficient-2 ghosts, and appending it to D5,6,7 gives the rank jump 60→61. Therefore the A2,d=4 scalar obstruction is killed by the continuation kernel.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains in the sweep, conditional on the already recorded residual/coefficient induction framework. The remaining task is expository: fold the verifier outputs and certificate blocks into a polished final proof appendix.

NEXT: Assemble the final proof package: cite the H1 Nielsen matrix, residual restricted-Lie induction, first coefficient-layer certificates, Koszul–Witt coefficient induction, the four nonlinear certificate blocks D.1–D.4, and the finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent.[TURN54-D3-MU4B4-STACKRANK-61-VERIFIED]
